output: Batch 9 completed. Removed the dead role-based tool registration helpers from `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, deleted `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` because it had no remaining external readers after that cleanup, and updated the two directly affected social-route tests to assert against `admin_tools.TOOLS` instead. Verification passed with `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header` and the full suite `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header`. Checkpoint written to `.megaplan/plans/follow-up-cleanup-pass-after-20260411-0608/execution_batch_9.json`.

task_updates.executor_notes: Removed `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, the two role-classification asserts, and `get_tools_for_role` from `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`. A fresh grep across `src` and `tests` showed `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` had no external readers once those helpers were removed, so it was deleted rather than rewritten. Updated the two affected assertions in `tests/test_social_route_tools.py` to derive registered tool names from `admin_tools.TOOLS` directly. Verification: `rg -n "MEMBER_TOOLS|ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS|get_tools_for_role|ALL_TOOL_NAMES" src tests` returned zero hits; `pytest tests/test_social_route_tools.py --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `27 passed, 4 warnings`; `pytest --tb=no -q --no-header` passed with `200 passed, 4 warnings`. `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS`, requester_id handling in `execute_tool`, and the query/batch/upsert implementations were left untouched.

sense_check_acknowledgments.executor_note: Confirmed. In `src/features/admin_chat/tools.py`, `MEMBER_TOOLS`, `ADMIN_ONLY_TOOLS`, the two role-classification asserts, and `get_tools_for_role` were removed. A fresh grep across `src` and `tests` showed `ALL_TOOL_NAMES` had no external readers after that cleanup, so it was deleted rather than rewritten. `_ADMIN_IDENTITY_INJECTED_TOOLS`, requester_id branches in `execute_tool`, and the query/batch/upsert tool implementations were left untouched.
